platforms.summary: Native-rich governance runtime. Strongest repo and local evidence for skills, hooks, subagents, MCP, and plugin-style installs.

trigger.userTrigger: Use Claude Code Agent/subagent surface.
trigger.autoTrigger: Meta-theory dispatch after Fetch/Thinking.
trigger.configTrigger: .claude/agents/*.md
trigger.hookTrigger: SubagentStart

capabilities.notes: Windows shell behavior differs across PowerShell, CMD, Git Bash, and WSL.

trigger.userTrigger: Explicit Agent/subagent request.
trigger.autoTrigger: Governed dispatch when workerTaskPackets are ready.
trigger.configTrigger: .claude/agents/*.md
trigger.hookTrigger: SubagentStart

capabilities.notes: Subagent context injection exists in repo hooks.

trigger.userTrigger: Name a custom agent or allow dispatch.
trigger.autoTrigger: Capability-first owner selection.
trigger.configTrigger: .claude/agents/*.md
trigger.hookTrigger: PreToolUse Agent gate

capabilities.notes: Durable owner remains canonical, runtime agent is projection.

trigger.userTrigger: Use skill trigger such as /meta-theory.
trigger.autoTrigger: Skill matching by description.
trigger.configTrigger: .claude/skills/*/SKILL.md
trigger.hookTrigger: Skill hook for meta-theory activation

capabilities.notes: Third-party skills must be read before enabling.

trigger.userTrigger: Slash command or CLI command.
trigger.autoTrigger: None unless governed dispatch selects it.
trigger.configTrigger: .claude/commands
trigger.hookTrigger: PreToolUse Bash

capabilities.notes: Shell quoting differs on Windows.

trigger.userTrigger: Type slash command.
trigger.autoTrigger: No automatic use.
trigger.configTrigger: .claude/commands/*.md
trigger.hookTrigger: UserPromptSubmit

capabilities.notes: User-triggered surface.

trigger.userTrigger: Lifecycle event.
trigger.autoTrigger: SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit/PreToolUse/PostToolUse/SubagentStart/Stop.
trigger.configTrigger: .claude/settings.json
trigger.hookTrigger: configured lifecycle

capabilities.notes: Strong lifecycle evidence; shell interpreter still OS-sensitive.

trigger.userTrigger: Use configured MCP tool.
trigger.autoTrigger: Tool selection by model.
trigger.configTrigger: .mcp.json
trigger.hookTrigger: None

capabilities.notes: MCP server command must be path-portable.

trigger.userTrigger: Use browser/web tool when available.
trigger.autoTrigger: Only after evidence lane selects it.
trigger.configTrigger: MCP/tool availability
trigger.hookTrigger: None

capabilities.notes: Use only when available in the host session.

trigger.userTrigger: Bash tool.
trigger.autoTrigger: Governed commands after planning.
trigger.configTrigger: permissions and hooks
trigger.hookTrigger: PreToolUse Bash

capabilities.notes: Shell differences must be probed for Win/Mac.

trigger.userTrigger: Read/Edit/Write tools.
trigger.autoTrigger: Execution worker actions.
trigger.configTrigger: permissions deny list
trigger.hookTrigger: PreToolUse/PostToolUse

capabilities.notes: Preserve user changes.

trigger.userTrigger: Edit tool.
trigger.autoTrigger: Scoped execution.
trigger.configTrigger: tool availability
trigger.hookTrigger: PreToolUse/PostToolUse Edit

capabilities.notes: Read-before-edit required.

trigger.userTrigger: Host permissions and hooks.
trigger.autoTrigger: None.
trigger.configTrigger: settings permissions
trigger.hookTrigger: PreToolUse deny

capabilities.notes: Not the same as Codex sandbox_mode.

trigger.userTrigger: Permission request or prompt choice.
trigger.autoTrigger: Tool permission boundary.
trigger.configTrigger: permissions
trigger.hookTrigger: permission hooks

capabilities.notes: Use for permissions, not product choices.

trigger.userTrigger: Configured host permission mode.
trigger.autoTrigger: Tool boundary.
trigger.configTrigger: settings permissions
trigger.hookTrigger: PreToolUse

capabilities.notes: Machine policy may differ.

trigger.userTrigger: Native choice/Elicitation when host supports it.
trigger.autoTrigger: Meta-theory choice gate.
trigger.configTrigger: none
trigger.hookTrigger: Elicitation if available

capabilities.notes: Fallback to chat decision card.

trigger.userTrigger: Assistant presents 2-4 options in chat.
trigger.autoTrigger: Choice policy fallback.
trigger.configTrigger: config/governance/choice-surface-policy.json
trigger.hookTrigger: None

capabilities.notes: Portable fallback.

trigger.userTrigger: Permission UI when host provides it.
trigger.autoTrigger: Permission request.
trigger.configTrigger: host policy
trigger.hookTrigger: permission hook

capabilities.notes: Not for ordinary product choices.

trigger.userTrigger: Shell/background helper when approved.
trigger.autoTrigger: Setup may start memory service.
trigger.configTrigger: setup.mjs
trigger.hookTrigger: SessionStart/Stop

capabilities.notes: Daemon behavior differs by OS.

trigger.userTrigger: MCP memory or hooks.
trigger.autoTrigger: SessionStart/Stop memory hooks.
trigger.configTrigger: .mcp.json; .claude/settings.json
trigger.hookTrigger: SessionStart/Stop

capabilities.notes: Probe output should not expose secrets.

trigger.userTrigger: Read graphify-out or graphify CLI.
trigger.autoTrigger: Graph hint hook before searches.
trigger.configTrigger: graphify install
trigger.hookTrigger: PreToolUse search hint

capabilities.notes: Graph may be stale; check built commit.

trigger.userTrigger: Open project.
trigger.autoTrigger: Host reads CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md.
trigger.configTrigger: CLAUDE.md
trigger.hookTrigger: None

capabilities.notes: Project rules are durable guidance.

trigger.userTrigger: Any project under global install.
trigger.autoTrigger: Host reads global config/memory.
trigger.configTrigger: ~/.claude
trigger.hookTrigger: global hooks

capabilities.notes: Global writes need approval.

trigger.userTrigger: Run setup/sync.
trigger.autoTrigger: npm run meta:sync.
trigger.configTrigger: config/sync.json
trigger.hookTrigger: None

capabilities.notes: Generated projection, not source of truth.

trigger.userTrigger: Run global install command.
trigger.autoTrigger: None without explicit request.
trigger.configTrigger: config/skills.json
trigger.hookTrigger: None

capabilities.notes: External action boundary applies.

trigger.userTrigger: Run dependency discovery.
trigger.autoTrigger: Fetch before owner selection.
trigger.configTrigger: dependency registry
trigger.hookTrigger: None

capabilities.notes: Read-only by default.

trigger.userTrigger: Skill inventory request.
trigger.autoTrigger: Fetch capability search.
trigger.configTrigger: config/skills.json
trigger.hookTrigger: None

capabilities.notes: Description quality affects matching.

trigger.userTrigger: Doctor hooks.
trigger.autoTrigger: Validation.
trigger.configTrigger: .claude/settings.json
trigger.hookTrigger: None

capabilities.notes: Trust review for non-managed hooks.

trigger.userTrigger: Lifecycle event or command.
trigger.autoTrigger: Hooks run on lifecycle.
trigger.configTrigger: .claude/settings.json
trigger.hookTrigger: SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit/PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop

capabilities.notes: Default mode may warn/progressive rather than block.

trigger.userTrigger: Approval or choice response.
trigger.autoTrigger: Permission boundary or choice policy.
trigger.configTrigger: choice policy/permissions
trigger.hookTrigger: permission or user prompt

capabilities.notes: Use when branch-changing choice cannot be inferred.

platforms.summary: Native governance runtime with AGENTS.md, project skills, hooks, MCP, sandbox/approval, and explicitly requested subagent support. Current session may override configured sandbox and approval.

trigger.userTrigger: User explicitly requests agent or a governed task requires it.
trigger.autoTrigger: Meta_Kim may select agent only after Fetch and Thinking identify it as a valid route component.
trigger.configTrigger: codex runtime projection and registry configuration.
trigger.hookTrigger: None unless the runtime maps it to a lifecycle event.

capabilities.notes: Codex support depends on host mode; subagents require explicit request and hooks require trust review.

trigger.userTrigger: Subagents must be explicitly requested by the user or by an explicit /meta-theory multi-agent task.

platforms.summary: Workspace-oriented runtime. Repo projection provides workspaces, skills, template MCP, and managed hook assets; Windows recommended path is WSL2 for many workflows.

trigger.hookTrigger: preToolUse remains tool governance only; ordinary choice confirmation uses the chat card fallback.

capabilities.notes: Cursor human confirmation is stable as a chat decision card loaded by alwaysApply rules, not as a verified native modal.

codex: Subagents must be explicitly requested or selected by a governed task; do not auto-spawn by default.
Inactive subagent approval may surface in interactive sessions.
Non-interactive sessions fail fresh approval actions that cannot present UI.
Default config is commonly workspace-write plus on-request, but current session may override it.
openclaw: Windows native support should be treated separately from WSL2.
Third-party skills require read-before-enable, allowlist, and sandbox preference.
Register onboard, doctor, and skills verify as verification commands when available.
cursor: Do not mark hooks or native choice as native without official or local proof.
Treat Cursor as a light governance projection platform until verified.
